reactions.name: Methane Combustion
reactions.description: The complete combustion of methane, a primary component of natural gas.

reactions.name: Ethane Combustion
reactions.description: The complete combustion of ethane.

reactions.name: Propane Combustion
reactions.description: The burning of propane, used in gas grills and home heating.

reactions.name: Butane Combustion
reactions.description: The combustion of butane, commonly found in lighters.

reactions.name: Pentane Combustion
reactions.description: The complete combustion of pentane.

reactions.name: Hexane Combustion
reactions.description: The complete combustion of hexane, a component of gasoline.

reactions.name: Octane Combustion
reactions.description: The combustion of octane, a major component of gasoline.

reactions.name: Methanol Combustion
reactions.description: The combustion of methanol, a simple alcohol fuel.

reactions.name: Ethanol Combustion
reactions.description: The burning of ethanol, an alcohol fuel.

reactions.name: Propanol Combustion
reactions.description: The combustion of propanol.

reactions.name: Glucose Combustion (Respiration)
reactions.description: The overall process of cellular respiration, releasing energy from glucose.

reactions.name: Acetylene Combustion
reactions.description: The combustion of acetylene, used in high-temperature welding torches.

reactions.name: Water Synthesis
reactions.description: The formation of water from hydrogen and oxygen gas.

reactions.name: Table Salt Formation
reactions.description: The reaction of sodium metal and chlorine gas to form sodium chloride.

reactions.name: Iron(III) Oxide Formation (Rust)
reactions.description: The oxidation of iron to form iron(III) oxide, commonly known as rust.

reactions.name: Haber-Bosch Process
reactions.description: An industrial process for producing ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen gas.

reactions.name: Sulfur Dioxide Formation
reactions.description: The burning of sulfur in oxygen to produce sulfur dioxide.

reactions.name: Magnesium Oxide Formation
reactions.description: The combustion of magnesium metal to form a bright white solid.

reactions.name: Slaked Lime Formation
reactions.description: The reaction of calcium oxide (quicklime) with water to form calcium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Aluminum Chloride Synthesis
reactions.description: The reaction of aluminum metal with chlorine gas.

reactions.name: Lithium Nitride Synthesis
reactions.description: The reaction of lithium metal with nitrogen gas.

reactions.name: Phosphorus Trichloride Synthesis
reactions.description: The reaction of white phosphorus with chlorine gas.

reactions.name: Sulfur Trioxide Formation
reactions.description: The key step in the Contact process for making sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Ammonia and HCl Reaction
reactions.description: The reaction of ammonia gas and hydrogen chloride gas to form solid ammonium chloride.

reactions.name: Potassium Oxide Synthesis
reactions.description: The formation of potassium oxide from potassium metal and oxygen.

reactions.name: Iron(II) Sulfide Synthesis
reactions.description: The direct combination of iron and sulfur upon heating.

reactions.name: Water Electrolysis
reactions.description: Decomposition of water into oxygen and hydrogen gas via an electric current.

reactions.name: Hydrogen Peroxide Decomposition
reactions.description: The breakdown of hydrogen peroxide into water and oxygen, often catalyzed.

reactions.name: Limestone Decomposition
reactions.description: The thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide.

reactions.name: Potassium Chlorate Decomposition
reactions.description: A common lab method for producing oxygen gas by heating potassium chlorate.

reactions.name: Mercury(II) Oxide Decomposition
reactions.description: The breakdown of mercury(II) oxide into liquid mercury and oxygen gas upon heating.

reactions.name: Carbonic Acid Decomposition
reactions.description: The unstable carbonic acid decomposing into water and carbon dioxide.

reactions.name: Baking Soda Decomposition
reactions.description: The thermal decomposition of sodium bicarbonate (baking soda).

reactions.name: Ammonium Dichromate Volcano
reactions.description: A dramatic decomposition reaction that produces chromium(III) oxide 'ash'.

reactions.name: Nitrogen Triiodide Decomposition
reactions.description: The highly sensitive explosive decomposition of nitrogen triiodide.

reactions.name: Copper(II) Hydroxide Decomposition
reactions.description: The decomposition of copper(II) hydroxide by heating.

reactions.name: Nitroglycerin Decomposition
reactions.description: The explosive decomposition of nitroglycerin.

reactions.name: Potassium Nitrate Decomposition
reactions.description: The thermal decomposition of potassium nitrate.

reactions.name: Zinc in Hydrochloric Acid
reactions.description: Zinc metal reacting with hydrochloric acid to produce hydrogen gas.

reactions.name: Iron in Copper(II) Sulfate
reactions.description: An iron nail placed in a copper(II) sulfate solution will be coated with copper.

reactions.name: Chlorine displaces Bromine
reactions.description: Chlorine gas bubbling through a potassium bromide solution.

reactions.name: Sodium in Water
reactions.description: The highly exothermic reaction of sodium metal in water.

reactions.name: Thermite Reaction
reactions.description: A highly exothermic reaction between iron(III) oxide and aluminum.

reactions.name: Copper in Silver Nitrate
reactions.description: A copper wire placed in silver nitrate solution forms silver crystals.

reactions.name: Magnesium in Sulfuric Acid
reactions.description: Magnesium reacting with sulfuric acid.

reactions.name: Fluorine displaces Chlorine
reactions.description: The most reactive halogen, fluorine, displaces chlorine.

reactions.name: Aluminum in Copper(II) Chloride
reactions.description: Aluminum foil reacting with a copper(II) chloride solution.

reactions.name: Potassium in Water
reactions.description: The violent reaction of potassium metal with water, often igniting the hydrogen produced.

reactions.name: Silver Halide Precipitation
reactions.description: A classic precipitation reaction forming solid silver chloride.

reactions.name: Lead Iodide Precipitation
reactions.description: Formation of a bright yellow precipitate of lead(II) iodide.

reactions.name: Acid-Base Neutralization
reactions.description: The reaction of a strong acid and a strong base to form salt and water.

reactions.name: Sulfuric Acid Neutralization
reactions.description: The neutralization of sulfuric acid with potassium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Barium Sulfate Precipitation
reactions.description: Formation of a dense white precipitate of barium sulfate.

reactions.name: Iron(III) Hydroxide Precipitation
reactions.description: Formation of a reddish-brown precipitate of iron(III) hydroxide.

reactions.name: Baking Soda and Vinegar
reactions.description: A classic acid-base reaction that produces carbon dioxide gas.

reactions.name: Calcium Carbonate with Acid
reactions.description: The reaction of an acid with a carbonate, producing a salt, water, and carbon dioxide.

reactions.name: Copper(II) Sulfate and Sodium Hydroxide
reactions.description: Formation of a blue precipitate of copper(II) hydroxide.

reactions.name: Phosphoric Acid Neutralization
reactions.description: The neutralization of phosphoric acid with calcium hydroxide.

reactions.name: Photosynthesis
reactions.description: The process used by plants to convert light energy into chemical energy.

reactions.name: Saponification (Soap Making)
reactions.description: A simplified representation of soap making, reacting fat (triglyceride) with lye.

reactions.name: Permanganate with HCl
reactions.equation: KMnO4 + HCl -> KCl + MnCl2 + H2O + Cl2
reactions.description: A redox reaction between potassium permanganate and hydrochloric acid.

reactions.name: Dichromate Redox Reaction
reactions.equation: K2Cr2O7 + H2SO4 + H2S -> K2SO4 + Cr2(SO4)3 + H2O + S
reactions.description: A redox reaction involving potassium dichromate as a strong oxidizing agent.

reactions.name: Water Gas Shift Reaction
reactions.description: An industrial reaction used to produce hydrogen gas from carbon monoxide and water.
